name: Artichoke and Red Pepper Pizza
recipeIngredient: 1 (10 oz.) Can Pillsbury Refrigerated All Purpose Pizza Crust
2 Red Bell Peppers, cut into 1/4-inch strips
1 (2.5 oz.) Jar Sliced Green Giant Mushrooms, drained
3 Tablespoons Chopped Fresh Basil or 1 tablespoon Dried Basil Leaves
1 (6 oz.) Jar Marinated Artichoke Hearts, drained and chopped
1 (2 1/4 oz.) Jar Sliced Ripe Olives, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F.
2. Grease a 13x9-inch (3-quart) baking dish or 12-inch pizza pan.
3. Unroll the dough and place it in the greased pan. Starting at the center, press the dough out with your hands.
4. Bake at 425°F for 8 to 11 minutes or until lightly golden brown.
5. In a food processor bowl with metal blade or blender container, process garlic for 10 to 15 seconds or until finely chopped. Transfer 1 tablespoon of oil to a separate dish.
6. With the machine running, add the remaining oil all at once through the feed tube or opening in the lid. Process for 20 to 30 seconds or until blended.
7. Spread the garlic mixture evenly over the partially baked crust.
8. Heat reserved 1 tablespoon oil in a medium skillet over medium-high heat until hot. Cook and stir peppers in the oil until crisp-tender, about 3 to 5 minutes.
9. Layer peppers, mushrooms, basil, artichokes, and olives over the garlic mixture. Top with cheeses.
10. Bake at 425°F for 8 to 14 minutes or until the crust is golden brown around the edges and the cheese is melted. Serve 8 servings.
